guidance.promptSnippet: Use todo only for genuinely multi-step work where a visible task list helps.
promptGuidelines: Use todo only when the request has 3 or more distinct, meaningful steps; multiple user-requested outcomes; explicit dependencies; broad repo-wide changes; or a long-running implementation/debugging session where progress would otherwise be hard to track.
Do not create todos for single straightforward tasks, trivial edits, short searches, one-command checks, small explanations, code review comments, or purely informational answers.
Do not create todos during early exploration just to mirror a generic investigate/implement/test workflow. If the scope becomes clearly multi-step after exploration, create a concise todo list then.
Do not create a one-item todo list. If fewer than 3 useful trackable items exist, do the work directly and use brief progress updates in chat instead.
Each todo must represent a user-visible outcome, not internal bookkeeping. Avoid filler items like investigate, implement, test unless each maps to a specific outcome.
Keep exactly one item in_progress while working. Mark items completed only after the work is actually done and verified.
When all requested work is complete, mark remaining items completed and clear the list if the tool supports clearing.
